title: Radiation Shield for Meteorological Temperature Sensors
content: A radiation shield (Stevenson screen or solar radiation shield) encloses a temperature or humidity sensor to prevent solar radiation heating errors while allowing free airflow for accurate ambient measurements.
extended_content: Solar heating can cause temperature errors of +5 to +30 °C without shielding.  A WMO-standard naturally-ventilated radiation shield (e.g., Gill multi-plate shield) reduces daytime radiation error to <0.5 °C at wind speeds > 1 m/s.  Forced-ventilation shields achieve <0.1 °C error but consume power (≈ 0.5–2 W fan).  Shield material should have high solar reflectivity (white UV-resistant ABS or polycarbonate, albedo > 0.85) and low thermal mass.  WMO height specification: sensor at 1.25–2 m above short grass.
source: WMO-No.8 Guide to Instruments, Ch.2; Richardson et al., 2009
